seoTitle: Fannie Bay vs Farrar — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fannie Bay and Farrar. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fannie Bay vs Farrar: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fannie Bay and Farrar.
heading: Fannie Bay vs Farrar — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fannie Bay, Northern Territory and Farrar, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fannie Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Farrar (32/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fannie Bay or Farrar safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fannie Bay has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Farrar scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fannie Bay scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Farrar scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fannie Bay or Farrar?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fannie Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
